module.name: client
module.purpose: Client crate for the user-facing session. event.rs defines ClientEvent, the user-facing event surface; session.rs implements ClientSession, a self-connecting client that mirrors HostSession's worker + shutdown model. new(name) stores the session name in a module-level SOURCE_NAME OnceLock, builds the user ClientEvent channel via build_channel(), initializes agent_tx as an empty SharedNetSender<SessionMessage> (Arc<Mutex<None>>), creates a fresh shutdown Arc<AtomicBool>, and then starts the worker itself by calling the private spawn_worker(agent_tx, user_tx, shutdown) - moving the user_tx sender half of the channel directly into the worker - and stores the returned JoinHandle in worker, so the session begins connecting to the agent as soon as it is constructed. is_connected() is a &self status query that locks the shared agent_tx (std::sync::Mutex, poison-tolerant) and returns whether the NetSender to the agent has been established (.is_some()), so callers poll it to learn when the connection is ready; the bounded wait that the session previously performed internally now lives in the marix-client binary. spawn_worker loads the global Config, parses config.client.core_address into a SocketAddr, and loops while not shut down: it connect_channel::<SessionMessage>(address) to the agent (retrying on connect error), stores the returned NetSender into the shared agent_tx, and runs run_worker on the returned NetReceiver; run_worker builds a current-thread tokio runtime and block_on's an async loop that recv()s each inbound SessionMessage, unwraps message.event and translates it via the private to_client_event, and sends any resulting ClientEvent through user_tx, breaking when shutdown is set. create_task builds a TaskSignature { name: request, id: TaskId::new() } (the client allocates the task id) and sends SessionEvent::Task(signature, TaskEvent::Create { request }) to the agent via the private send_to_agent, while cancel_task builds a TaskSignature keyed by the given TaskId and sends SessionEvent::Task(signature, TaskEvent::Cancel); send_to_agent locks the shared agent_tx (std::sync::Mutex, poison-tolerant) and, when a connection is present, packages the SessionEvent into a SessionMessage via the static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage (which stamps the SOURCE_NAME) before try_send. to_client_event maps only content-bearing inbound task events to ClientEvent::Common(String) - TaskEvent::Status(TaskStatus::Update { content }), TaskEvent::Status(TaskStatus::Failed { reason }), TaskEvent::Preview { content }, and TaskEvent::CreateFailed { reason } - and returns None for lifecycle-only task statuses (Started/Canceled/Succeed) and all execution events, so they are not surfaced. receiver() borrows the user ClientEvent receiver end (&Receiver<ClientEvent>) so the caller (for example the CLI) can consume it with recv(), and close() sets the shutdown Arc<AtomicBool> flag (Ordering::Relaxed) and, if present, takes and joins the worker JoinHandle. All struct fields are private (agent_tx, user_rx, worker, shutdown); the user_tx sender half is no longer a struct field, since new() moves it into the worker.     client/Cargo.toml now depends on tokio (net, rt-multi-thread, sync, time) to drive the current-thread recv runtime; lib.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only. The crate now also ships an executable entry point: main.rs is the marix-client binary that loads the global Config (exiting on error), constructs marix_client::ClientSession::new(config.name) (which immediately starts the worker and begins connecting), then polls session.is_connected() in a bounded wait loop (up to ~5 seconds: 100 iterations of a 50ms sleep) until the connection is ready before proceeding, then either runs a one-shot prompt from CLI args or an interactive stdin loop, creating a task per prompt via create_task and draining user events through the file-local private drain_events helper (session.receiver().recv_timeout(30s)) that prints each ClientEvent::Common(message) until idle or disconnect, and calls close() on exit; client/Cargo.toml now declares the marix-client [[bin]] target alongside the existing [lib].
